props.caption: The open agent layer acquires institutions

items.note: A tit-for-tat tested the Lake Lucerne ceasefire without breaking it. On 25 June an Iranian drone struck the Singapore-flagged container ship Ever Lovely in Hormuz; on 26 June US Central Command struck Iranian missile and drone storage and coastal radar sites in reply, calling Tehran's attack a ceasefire violation. Trump called it a foolish violation, Vance said violence will be met with violence, and the IRGC warned its next response would be more extensive. No casualties were reported, and the strikes spared the nuclear sites.

Israel, Lebanon and the United States signed a trilateral framework in Washington on 26 June: a structured process to disarm Hezbollah, a Military Coordination Group, and Lebanese army control restored pending verified disarmament. Rubio called it the beginning of the beginning. Netanyahu said Israel will hold the southern security zone as long as Hezbollah has not disarmed, so the signed document and the tanks on the ground point in different directions.

El Obeid remains inside the prevention window while the bombardment grinds on. The UN Security Council warned of an imminent risk of mass atrocities as the RSF massed around the North Kordofan capital and drones kept striking the city. A full ground assault inside El Obeid has not been confirmed, so the verified picture is reinforcement and air attack, and the protective action still lags the warning.

items.what: The IAEA inspection dispute stayed gridlocked: Grossi insists inspections of the bombed sites will happen, Iran's foreign ministry says there are no plans for visits and that cooperation runs through its own institutions. Rubio flagged technical talks for 29-30 June, so the promise-versus-denial standoff continues with no inspectors on site.

Brent slid toward the low $70s on Friday as Hormuz supply fears eased, then the weekend strikes reintroduced a risk premium the screen had just unwound. Korean chip names led a sharp tech selloff, while the dollar held roughly flat.

Days before OpenAI hired its creator, OpenClaw's skill marketplace was found riddled with malicious packages by Palo Alto's Unit 42, including a class of attack that weaponizes the agent's own tool-use. The consolidation around OpenAI, Nvidia and Prosus is also a bet that institutions can secure what the viral phase could not.

The disputed killing of Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah remained unresolved, with Israel's evidence set against the network's account of a targeted journalist, as strikes continued through the fragile post-ceasefire period.
Kyiv's long-range campaign kept reaching the energy and supply infrastructure behind the front, with strikes reported on Russian gas and satellite-linked sites even as seaborne crude flows held up.
